summary: Large decomp/matching pass over the menu diagram files, mainly src/melee/mn/mndiagram.c and src/melee/mn/mndiagram3.c. The PR matched mnDiagram_PopupAnimProc completely, substantially improved mnDiagram_InputProc and fn_802461BC, added typed overlays for previously raw data tables, and adjusted headers/assertions/control flow to better reproduce the original codegen. Evidence is mostly from the diff and decomp.dev bot report; the PR body and human review comments were empty.

key_files.reason: Largest change; added mnDiagram_AnimTable overlay, rewrote/improved mnDiagram_InputProc navigation logic, matched mnDiagram_PopupAnimProc, and improved several popup/cursor/detail routines.
Added/improved mndiagram3 detail/list input handling including fn_802461BC, replaced raw table accesses with typed tables, and introduced fake inline JObj translate setters for matching.
Adjusted assertions, AnimLoopSettings usage, and detail/stat row code; bot reported some regressions here despite sdata2 improvement.
Promoted raw extern byte arrays to mnDiagram3_PosTable and mnDiagram3_StatTable with offset-preserving fields.
Changed mnDiagram2_803EEB60 from a single AnimLoopSettings extern to an AnimLoopSettings[2] and removed raw assert-string externs.

what_changed: Added a local typed overlay mnDiagram_AnimTable over mnDiagram_803EE728 so popup animation/text layout code can access Point3d entries and trailing AnimLoopSettings at fixed offsets instead of using char* arithmetic.

Reworked mnDiagram_PopupAnimProc to use mnDiagram_AnimTable, Vec3 temporaries, an inline helper returning &arg0->points[arg1], explicit y/z temporaries, and tbl->x64.end_frame; decomp.dev reported it became a new 100% match.

Expanded mnDiagram_InputProc from simple button handling into much more explicit name/fighter navigation code, including count checks, hovered_selection clamping, sorted_fighters/sorted_names traversal, unlocked fighter filtering, GetNameText filtering, and many goto-shaped loops matching expected control flow.
Adjusted sorting helpers mnDiagram_8023FA6C and mnDiagram_8023FC28 with stack padding, u8 casts, an mnDiagram_Assets pointer, and more explicit unlocked/null-name tie conditions.
Added HSD_ASSERTREPORT/HSD_ASSERT usage in mndiagram.c, mndiagram2.c, and mndiagram3.c, including replacing hand-written OSReport/__assert string-table access in at least mnDiagram3_80247008.
In mndiagram3.static.h, introduced mnDiagram3_PosTable for mnDiagram3_803EEC28 and mnDiagram3_StatTable for mnDiagram3_803EEC4C, replacing u8 array extern declarations.

In mndiagram3.c, replaced raw jobj translation field manipulation in mnDiagram3_8024714C with HSD_JObjGetTranslation* calls and fake inline HSD_JObjSetTranslateX/Y/Z helpers that include jobj.h-style asserts and ftCo_800C6AFC invalidation behavior.

Added fn_802461BC implementation for mndiagram3 input/list handling, covering cancel/back, page/menu transitions, name/fighter mode switching, row label destruction/recreation, saved_selection movement, scroll_offset movement, and detail-view refresh.
In mndiagram2.static.h, changed mnDiagram2_803EEB60 to AnimLoopSettings mnDiagram2_803EEB60[2], reflecting table-like use rather than a single struct.
decomp.dev bot reported overall matched code unchanged at 69.13% but +184 bytes, with one new match and multiple large unmatched-item improvements.
smart_moves: Using a typed overlay struct for data that was already laid out at fixed offsets made popup code more readable while preserving exact offset-sensitive access patterns.
The mnDiagram_PopupAnimProc inline helper is a small codegen lever: it expresses simple point indexing while likely preserving the original call/address computation shape needed for the match.
The mndiagram3 PosTable/StatTable header types remove ambiguous u8-array casts and document the real table contents without overnaming unknown trailing bytes.
Fake inline JObj setters in mndiagram3 reproduce the assert line numbers and matrix invalidation side effect of HSD_JObj setters while allowing local codegen control.
The rewritten navigation code keeps original-like goto labels and sentinel values such as 0x78 and 0x19, which is appropriate for matching code that scans sparse name/fighter lists.
Clamping hovered_selection when switching between name and fighter mode avoids stale UI positions after changing to a list with fewer visible entries, while also matching more of the original logic.

The PR improved important high-value unmatched functions rather than only chasing small data changes: mnDiagram_InputProc rose from 22.71% to 89.53%, and fn_802461BC from 0.00% to 85.31% according to the bot report.
decomp_lessons: For menu data blobs, first identify repeated Vec3/Point3d and AnimLoopSettings regions and create offset-annotated overlay structs instead of continuing with raw char* arithmetic.
When a raw extern byte array is actually a table, moving the type into the static header can improve both call-site casts and downstream matching work.
Do not remove unknown trailing bytes prematurely; fields like x50/x68/x78 and x40/x58/x64 keep the layout honest while allowing known subfields to be typed.
Original menu input code may use flattened/goto-heavy scans over sorted arrays and unlocked/name-valid filters; structured loops can be less readable to the compiler and worse for matching.
Small integer type details matter in this code: u8 casts on indexes and sentinel values are visible in codegen and were used in sorting/navigation changes.
HSD assert macros and __FILE__ context can be matching-critical. Replacing ad hoc OSReport/__assert sequences with HSD_ASSERTREPORT or recreating jobj.h inline assert context can improve equivalence.
A PR can be merge-worthy despite local regressions if it substantially advances important unmatched regions, but future work should track any broken 100% matches introduced by helper/header changes.
naming_conventions: New overlay/data-table types used subsystem prefixes and descriptive suffixes: mnDiagram_AnimTable, mnDiagram3_PosTable, mnDiagram3_StatTable.
Unknown struct fields retained offset-style names such as x40, x58, x64, x70, x88, x94, x50, x68, and x78.
Known data table members were named by role where evidence was clear, such as points and indices.
Local variables were sometimes renamed for clarity and matching intent, for example user_data in place of data in allocation/init paths.
Matching-only helper shims were explicitly marked with a Fake suffix: HSD_JObjSetTranslateX_Fake, HSD_JObjSetTranslateY_Fake, HSD_JObjSetTranslateZ_Fake.
Goto labels in the reconstructed input logic encode direction/mode, such as nc_outer/nc_inner, nr_outer/nr_inner, fc_outer/fc_inner, fr_outer/fr_inner, up_n, dn_n_find, lf_n, rt_n_find, and up_f.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK changes, including PAD_STACK(16), PAD_STACK(80), and PAD_STACK(0x18), to tune stack layout.
Introduced temporary variables for f32 y/z and HSD_Text* assignments to influence store order in popup text positioning.
Used explicit u8 casts in array indexing and state packing to match narrow integer behavior.
Replaced direct byte-offset casts with typed pointer overlays while preserving exact offsets through struct padding.
Kept goto-based loops for sparse list traversal rather than simplifying into higher-level loops.
Used HSD_ASSERTREPORT and HSD_ASSERT to reproduce original assertion paths and string/file-line behavior.
Temporarily redefined __FILE__ to "jobj.h" around fake JObj translate inline helpers, then restored it to "mndiagram3.c".
Used HSD_JObjGetTranslationX/Y/Z accessors plus fake setters to control inline expansion and side effects around popup row positioning.
Maintained sentinel return/index values such as 0x78 for names and 0x19 for fighters when scans run past valid ranges.

review_feedback.feedback: Reported one new full match: main/melee/mn/mndiagram mnDiagram_PopupAnimProc improved by +131 bytes from 74.29% to 100.00%.
Reported one broken match: main/melee/mn/mndiagram3 mnDiagram3_80247008 regressed by -10 bytes from 100.00% to 97.22%.
Reported major unmatched improvements including fn_802461BC +2514 bytes to 85.31%, mnDiagram_InputProc +2098 bytes to 89.53%, mnDiagram_802427B4 to 81.61%, and mnDiagram_80242C0C to 92.70%.
Reported regressions in unmatched items, notably mnDiagram2_CreateStatRow -108 bytes, mnDiagram3_8024714C -32 bytes, and mnDiagram3_80245BA4 -14 bytes.
No human review comments or explanatory PR body were present in the slice, so reviewer intent is not available.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that restore mnDiagram3_80247008 to 100% after the regression introduced here.
Search for additional uses of fake HSD_JObjSetTranslate*_Fake helpers or __FILE__="jobj.h" matching tactics in menu code.
Look for subsequent cleanup or naming improvements to mnDiagram_AnimTable, mnDiagram3_PosTable, and mnDiagram3_StatTable unknown fields.
Investigate later mndiagram2 PRs that recover mnDiagram2_CreateStatRow and mnDiagram2_UpdateScrollArrows regressions.
Compare mnDiagram_InputProc later revisions to see whether the goto-heavy navigation reconstruction was preserved or simplified after matching improved.
